setting.note: Wagle's Nepal is split between a capital of cafés, galleries and easel painting and a countryside torn by the Maoist People's War. A painter named Drishya moves through Thamel, the old Durbar Square and the hill above the valley, then travels west into the war zone where he meets Palpasa; the violence he witnesses there follows him back to Kathmandu. The map runs from the city out to the mid-western hills that were the heartland of the insurgency.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Narayan Wagle, first published in Nepali in 2005. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Palpasa Café, Narayan Wagle (2005). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The burnt terracotta of Kathmandu brickwork and a painter's earth pigment. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
